props.message:
The Sigilkeep.

The great door is sealed. A line of wardstones glows across the threshold, dim. Khorgaul's hoard pulses behind them.

Carved into the wardstone:

"This door opens for one who has shared three Sigils. Bring proof of giving."

The wards do not stir.

A wardstone in the wall, fingers-of-the-Source carved deep:

"What is sealed here is not the Hoarder, but the shape of his hunger. The Sigilkeep waits for one who has learned not to want."

root.speaker: Keeper Maren
root.text: Welcome to the Sigilkeep, traveler. The wards here have held a thousand seasons. They thin now, but they hold. Speak — what would you ask of the keep?

choices.label: Tell me of the great door.

trig.speaker: Keeper Maren
trig.text: Three sigils, three vows, one design. Sight, Shaping, Sharing. The Triglyph is the keep's heart and the realm's lock. Bring all three to the door, and the keep opens. Bring two, and the keep weeps. Bring one, and the keep waits.

choices.label: Tell me of the great door.

door.speaker: Keeper Maren
door.text: The great door is the wound and the seal. It opens for one who bears the full Triglyph. None has passed it in living memory. But the wards thin, and one will come. I think one is here.

ward.speaker: Keeper Maren
ward.text: Wardstones drink the Source and breathe it as light. When the realm is bright, they hum. When the Hoarder gathers, they go silent. Listen at one and you will know the season.

cov.speaker: Keeper Maren
cov.text: Lirien made the realm and gave it freely. The covenant is that we receive freely and give freely. The keep is built upon that vow. When the giving stops, the keep falls.

bless.speaker: Keeper Maren
bless.text:
*touches your brow with a Source-cool finger*
Go with sight. Go with shaping. Go with sharing. Bring back the third sigil, traveler. Bring it home.

root.speaker: Acolyte Pyl
root.text:
*kneeling, eyes closed*
Forgive me — I am at prayer. ...One moment.

*opens eyes*
There. The vow is renewed. Were you wishing something of me?

choices.label: Why come so far to pray?

pray.speaker: Acolyte Pyl
pray.text: For the third sigil to be carried truly. For a small one to do a great thing. For the Hoarder's hand to be lifted, even one finger. Small prayers; but the Source listens to small things.

choices.label: Why come so far to pray?

far.speaker: Acolyte Pyl
far.text: The keep is the loudest place to pray. The wardstones carry it on. A prayer spoken here travels every wardstone in the realm by morning. Or so my teacher said. I have not asked the wardstones to confirm.

bye.speaker: Acolyte Pyl
bye.text: May the green hold you, traveler.
